title: Text Tag Basics
description: Learn the basic tags for headings, paragraphs, emphasis, and more.

levels.title: Your First Heading
levels.learningPoint: h1 represents the most important heading on a page
levels.instructions: Which tag should you use to make this text the main heading of a page?
levels.hint: h1 stands for Heading 1. It's the largest and most important heading.
levels.successMessage: Correct! h1 is used for the single most important heading on a page.

levels.title: Heading Hierarchy
levels.learningPoint: h1-h6 represent heading levels. Lower number = higher importance
levels.instructions: Create a subheading below the main heading.
levels.hint: The next level heading after h1 has a number one higher.
levels.successMessage: h2 is the subheading under h1. The hierarchy goes h1 > h2 > h3 and so on.

levels.title: Making a Paragraph
levels.learningPoint: The p tag represents a paragraph of text
levels.instructions: Wrap this sentence in a paragraph tag.
levels.hint: Think of the first letter of 'paragraph'.
levels.successMessage: p stands for paragraph. Use it to wrap blocks of text.

levels.title: Emphasis
levels.learningPoint: strong marks important text, em marks emphasized text
levels.instructions: Which tag should you use to mark a word as semantically important?
levels.hint: strong conveys semantic importance, not just visual boldness.
levels.successMessage: strong conveys semantic importance. b only makes text bold visually.

levels.title: Line Break & Divider
levels.learningPoint: br is a line break, hr is a thematic divider. Both are self-closing
levels.instructions: Add a horizontal rule (hr) between the two paragraphs.
levels.hint: hr stands for horizontal rule. It's self-closing: just write <hr>.
levels.successMessage: hr creates a thematic break between sections. It's a void element with no closing tag.

levels.title: Heading + Paragraph Combo
levels.learningPoint: Combine headings and paragraphs to structure your content
levels.instructions: Write an h2 heading 'About Me' followed by a p paragraph 'Hello, I am a student.'
levels.hint: Write h2 for the heading first, then p for the content right below.
levels.successMessage: Heading + paragraph is the most basic content structure. Real web pages start just like this!
